data.title: CapsoVision, Inc. reports Q4 results
data.description:
• CapsoVision, Inc. press release (CV): Q4
  • Revenue of $3.9M (+13.0% Y/Y) misses by $0.4M.
  • Fourth quarter 2025 gross profit was $2.0 million, an increase of $0.2 million, or 13%, compared to the fourth quarter of 2024. 
  • Fourth quarter 2025 operating expenses were $9.4 million, a $3.0 million increase from the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • Cash and cash equivalents totaled $10.1 million as of December 31, 2025.

data.title: Madrigal Pharmaceuticals gains amid renewed takeover speculation
data.description:
Madrigal Pharmaceuticals (MDGL) rose 12% amid renewed speculation it could be a takeover target, according to traders, who cited a Betaville "uncooked" alert that was circulating on Thursday. 

Betaville reported in January 2025 that Madrigal (MDGL) had hired advisers for talks with a potential buyer.  Madrigal (MDGL) has a market cap of $12 billion.

Madrigal (MDGL) has short interest of 16%.

data.title: Oxford Industries GAAP EPS of -$0.48 misses by $0.52, revenue of $374.5M beats by $2.66M
data.description:
• Oxford Industries press release (OXM): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.48 misses by $0.52.
  • Revenue of $374.5M (-4.1% Y/Y) beats by $2.66M.
  • Royalties and other operating income decreased $4 million to $16 million for the full year primarily due to decreased royalty income in Tommy Bahama reflecting the lower sales of licensing partners.
  • Full-year net loss was $28 million in fiscal 2025, compared to net earnings of $93 million in the prior year. For the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025, net loss was $7 million compared to net earnings of $18 million in the prior year.
  • Full-year EBITDA was $36 million in fiscal 2025, compared to $187 million in the prior year. On an adjusted basis, full-year EBITDA was $107 million compared to $193 million in the prior year. For the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025, adjusted EBITDA was $8 million compared to $38 million in the prior year, while adjusted EBITDA was $14 million in fiscal 2025 and $40 million in the prior year.
  • As a result of interim impairment assessments performed in the third quarter of fiscal 2025, the Company recognized noncash impairment charges totaling $61 million, primarily related to the Johnny Was trademark.
  • Interest expense increased to $7 million from $2 million in the prior year period primarily due to higher average outstanding debt during fiscal 2025 than the prior year.
  • The effective tax rate for fiscal 2025 was 27% compared to 20% in the prior year. The effective tax rate for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025 was 27% compared to 8% for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2024. The effective tax rates for both the full year and fourth quarter of fiscal 2025 were higher than a typical effective tax rate of 25% and included certain unfavorable discrete items that are not expected to recur in future periods.
  •

data.title: Fed should stay on sidelines amid market uncertainty - Goldman's Robert Kaplan
data.description:
Robert Kaplan, a senior figure at Goldman Sachs, said the Federal Reserve should continue its current approach of doing nothing as markets grapple with heightened uncertainty surrounding ongoing geopolitical tensions.

“For the moment, they should do nothing, which is what they are doing,” Kaplan said in a recent Bloomberg interview. “They should let this situation continue to evolve, keep monitoring it.”

Kaplan suggested that markets may gain more clarity within three to four weeks but cautioned that the path forward remains unclear. He noted that equity markets (SP500) (SPY) and credit spreads have remained relatively resilient because investors are pricing in a swift resolution to current tensions.

“The market is pricing in that there’s going to be a resolution,” Kaplan said. “That could change, though, if it’s clear that this is gonna go on longer.”

The Goldman executive also pushed back on concerns about economic weakness heading into the current period of turbulence. He said the firm’s forecast 30 days ago called for a strengthening U.S. economy with gross domestic product growth of 2.5% or higher.

“We did not see a cyclical weakening going into” the war, Kaplan said. “This may create a cyclical weakening, but it’s too soon to say that in my opinion.”

For investors seeking safe havens, Kaplan recommended taking a long-term approach rather than reacting to short-term volatility. He noted that traditional safe assets like gold (XAUUSD:CUR) (GLD) and the 10-year Treasury (US10Y) have behaved in surprising ways recently, with the precious metal and bond prices both dropping.

“Sometimes the best thing to do, and history has shown the best thing to do in this kind of period, is do nothing,” he said.

data.title: Newsmax Inc. reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Newsmax Inc. press release (NMAX): Q4 Revenue of $52.2M beats by $8.21M.

  • Newsmax reported a quarterly Net Loss of $(3.0) million as compared to a net loss of $(6.9) million reported in same quarter in the prior year, primarily driven by higher strategic investments in headcount, programming and production capabilities to support the ongoing expansion and enhancement of our content offering, stock-based compensation costs, offset by higher broadcasting advertising, affiliate fees, book sales and licensing revenue.

  • Quarterly adjusted EBITDA was $(1.3) million, a decrease of $(3.8) million from the amount reported in the same quarter last year, primarily due to higher production and programming expense, increased personnel, increase legal, consulting and public company costs. (See reconciliation of net loss to adjusted EBITDA below).

  • The Company ended the quarter with $131.3 million in cash and short-term investments. cash and cash equivalents were $20.4 million and short-term investments were $110.9 million.

  •

data.title: Datacentrex announces proposed public offering
data.description:
• Datacentrex (DTCX) on Thursday said it has commenced a public offering of common stock and pre-funded warrants to purchase common stock, subject to market conditions.
  • The company said it intends to use the proceeds for working capital and general corporate purposes, but did not disclose the size or pricing of the offering.
  • The offering is being made under an effective shelf registration filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.
  • DTCX -9.67% after hours to $2.8.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nasdaq dropped more than 2% as investors react to rising Middle East tensions
data.description:
Wall Street ended lower on Thursday as investors kept a close eye on escalating U.S.-Iran tensions after Tehran dismissed a U.S. proposal aimed at ending the conflict.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) closed lower by 2.3%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) finished down -1.7%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) handed back -1%

From a sector-by-sector standpoint, eight of the 11 S&P segments closed in the red, with communication services at the bottom. At the other end, energy was the strongest performer of the session. 

Treasury yields rose across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) climbed 9 basis points to 4.42%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) added 10 basis points to 3.99%. The 30-year yield (US30Y) edged up 4 basis points to 4.94%. 

A senior political-security official has reportedly told Iranian news network Press TV that Iran has formally rejected the American proposal, insisting that Tehran will dictate the terms and timing of the war’s conclusion. 

"The market is struggling to make sense of Iran War headlines. While nobody seems to be able to judge just how likely a ceasefire is, most are fully aware that a prolonged spike in crude oil increases the stagflation risk,” Seeking Alpha analyst Leo Nelissen stated.

On the economic front, initial jobless claims data for the week ended March 21 rose by 5K to 210K, against the consensus of 210K. 

As for stocks that were on the move, Best Buy (BBY) climbed +4.6%, and shares of Applovin Corporation (APP) fell -10.5%.

What do you think is next for Social Security? Share your opinion in our user poll.

data.title: SA Analyst: Traders are struggling to interpret Iran war headlines
data.description:
Wall Street closed sharply lower on Thursday as geopolitical uncertainty surrounding escalating U.S.-Iran tensions rattled investor sentiment and weighed heavily on risk assets.

Markets struggled to find direction after Tehran rejected a U.S. proposal aimed at de-escalating the conflict, heightening concerns about a prolonged standoff and its broader economic implications. 

The technology-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) led the declines, falling 2.3%, as weakness across growth stocks intensified selling pressure. The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) dropped 1.7%, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) shed 1%, reflecting broad-based losses across sectors. 

Energy markets remained a central focus for investors, as fears of supply disruptions pushed oil prices higher and reignited inflation concerns. Rising crude prices have the potential to complicate the global economic outlook, particularly if elevated costs persist and feed through to consumer prices. 

“The market is struggling to make sense of Iran war headlines,” said Seeking Alpha analyst Leo Nelissen. “

"While nobody seems to be able to judge just how likely a ceasefire is, most are fully aware that a prolonged spike in crude oil increases the stagflation risk.” 

Market Tracking ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

data.title: H.B. Fuller outlines $4.55–$4.90 EPS guidance and mid-single digit revenue growth amid supply chain volatility
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: H.B. Fuller Company (FUL) Q1 2026

Management View

  • CEO Celeste Mastin reported "we delivered on our profit commitment and executed with discipline in a challenging operating environment." Mastin highlighted that organic revenue decreased 6.6% year-on-year, with positive pricing offset by lower volume, but EBITDA of $119 million increased 4% year-on-year and EBITDA margin expanded 90 basis points to 15.4%.
  • Mastin detailed that the Engineering Adhesives (EA) segment achieved 3% organic growth (excluding solar), while HHC declined 10%, and BAS sales decreased 5.1%. Segment EBITDA margins improved, particularly in EA and HHC due to restructuring savings and positive price actions.
  • Mastin described significant supply chain disruptions from the Middle East conflict, noting, "we have received over 40 force majeure letters from suppliers in recent weeks, clear evidence that this is a major disruption." In response, H.B. Fuller announced "a minimum 10% price increase across all product lines globally effective April 1 with significantly higher price adjustments for certain technologies and regions where cost escalation is more acute."
  • Mastin emphasized that "this disruption creates a unique opportunity to support existing customers and gain market share, positioning us for improved volume growth in the future."
  • CFO John Corkrean stated, "Adjusted gross profit margin was 31.3%, up 170 basis points versus last year as positive pricing and raw material actions as well as restructuring savings more than offset volume declines." Corkrean also reported "adjusted earnings per share of $0.57 was up 6% versus the same quarter in 2025 driven by higher operating income and lower shares outstanding."
  • Corkrean shared, "Net debt to adjusted EBITDA was 3.1x, consistent with fiscal year-end 2025 and down from 3.5x at the end of the first quarter of last year."

Outlook

  • Corkrean announced, "Net revenue is now expected to be up mid-single digits and organic revenue is now expected to be up low single digits versus fiscal 2025, reflecting updated pricing actions and anticipated market share gains."
  • Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2026 is now expected to be in the range of $645 million to $675 million. Adjusted EPS is forecasted in the range of $4.55 to $4.90. For the second quarter, net revenue is expected to be up low single digits and adjusted EBITDA is expected in the range of $175 million to $185 million.
  • Corkrean indicated a temporary pause on closing acquisitions, reallocating capital to share repurchases while maintaining a target of 2.5x to 3x net debt to EBITDA.

Financial Results

  • Organic revenue decreased 6.6% year-on-year; pricing was up 0.6%, volume down 7.2%.
  • Currency had a positive impact of 3.6% and acquisitions added 0.7% to revenue.
  • Adjusted SG&A expense was up 4% year-over-year, but excluding acquisitions and foreign exchange, SG&A was down slightly year-on-year.
  • Cash flow from operations improved $49 million year-on-year.

Q&A

  • Patrick Cunningham, Citigroup: Asked about the 10% price hike and guidance raise amid raw material constraints. Mastin explained price increases are a response to "the market's really squeezed" and that "we believe this market reset is a very sustainable place for us to be," noting long-term supply chain changes.
  • Michael Harrison, Seaport: Asked about Lunar New Year’s impact and activity in China. Mastin said, "we experienced about a $15 million to $20 million revenue impact from Chinese New Year in Q1" and noted post-holiday, "our HHC business did very well in Asia Pacific."
  • Lucas Beaumont, UBS: Inquired about further pricing actions. Mastin confirmed, "10% was a minimum that we needed to do across all materials," and additional price supplements are being negotiated.
  • Kevin McCarthy, Vertical: Sought detail on share gain opportunities. Mastin emphasized "quite a number of opportunities to gain share in HHC, certainly, particularly because a lot of the raw material base for HHC comes out of China," and Corkrean added, "we are asking them to sign longer-term agreements."
  • Jeffrey Zekauskas, JPMorgan: Asked about volume outlook changes. Mastin cited "positive impact of gaining share" offset by "customers potentially not being able to get other materials" and "erosion in global demand because of this inflationary environment."
  • Emily Fusco, Deutsche Bank: Questioned order trends in March. Mastin reported "improved -- higher revenue" and "improved margins" in March, with no evidence of customer hoarding.
  • Rosemarie Morbelli, Gabelli: Asked about medical-grade adhesives. Mastin said, "our medical business in Europe was up almost 20%, again, organically," but noted the segment remains small. On solar, Mastin stated, "we're already at the trough revenue we expect there," with recovery expected by the third quarter.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed a proactive and occasionally skeptical tone, pressing on sustainability of price hikes, volume declines, and supply chain risks.
  • Management’s tone was confident during prepared remarks, repeatedly referencing discipline, agility, and market opportunity, while in Q&A, responses showed candor about uncertainties, but maintained a constructive outlook. Mastin used phrases such as "we believe this market reset is a very sustainable place for us to be" and "we're being very selective about where to gain share."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s sentiment shifted to a more urgent and opportunity-seeking tone, driven by supply chain disruptions and aggressive pricing.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance raised from flat to up 2% net revenue (previous quarter) to mid-single digit growth; organic revenue guidance moved from flat to low single digits.
  • Adjusted EPS guidance increased from a range of $4.35 to $4.70 to $4.55 to $4.90, and EBITDA guidance lifted from $630 million–$660 million to $645 million–$675 million.
  • Previous quarter focused on portfolio repositioning and cost structure, while the current quarter underscores rapid response to supply chain disruptions and market share capture.
  • Analysts in the current quarter are more focused on the sustainability of price and volume assumptions amid external shocks.
  • Management’s tone has shifted from steady progress to assertive action, highlighting supply chain resilience and market share as key priorities.

Risks and Concerns

  • Mastin warned of "significant broad-based inflationary pressure and raw material shortages" resulting from the Middle East conflict.
  • Over 40 force majeure notices have been received from suppliers, signaling major disruption in raw material supply.
  • Corkrean indicated managing inventory will be "a little trickier in this environment," and that the "biggest question will probably be around inventory management."
  • Mastin anticipated potential customer challenges in sourcing non-adhesive inputs, which could negatively impact demand, though no such impact was seen yet.

Final Takeaway

H.B. Fuller management delivered an assertive message that, despite lower volumes and supply chain disruptions, decisive early pricing actions and a robust global sourcing strategy are expected to preserve profitability and support mid-single digit revenue growth for 2026. The company is leveraging current market volatility to pursue share gains, maintain margin expansion, and strengthen long-term competitiveness, while proactively managing risks associated with raw material shortages and inflation.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Macy's sees strong results from tests of its Gemini-powered chatbot
data.description:
Macy's (M) saw positive results from early tests of its Google Gemini-powered chatbot.

Notably, Macy's (M) customers who use the Ask Macy's chatbot have spent around 4.75 times more than those who don’t, according to company data over the last few weeks. The department store operator is testing the shopping assistant with about half of the visitors to its website.

The AI shopping assistant powered by Google’s Gemini platform lets shoppers ask product questions in natural language and get responses without leaving the Macy’s site. Gemini interprets the intent, and Macy’s (M) returns relevant product guidance or answers. 

The retail sector in general has seen strong results from the implementation of AI chatbots. Conversion rates on shopping sites have improved, and some companies have been able to reduce headcount dedicated to customer service.

Shares of Macy's (M) dipped 0.5% in Thursday trading and are down more than 16% on a year-to-date basis.
